text.title: api-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for api-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Asserting only on status_code without validating the response body.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A 200 with a missing required field is a schema regression. Always validate body against the OpenAPI spec.

failure-mode.description: Weakening assertions to fix a failing test (assert r.status_code in (200, 201, 204)).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Loosened assertions hide real regressions. Failures must be fixed in production code or the spec, not in the test assertion.

failure-mode.description: Sharing test fixtures across parallel test runs with pytest-xdist without isolation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Shared DB state causes non-deterministic failures. Use pytest.mark.serial or --dist=loadgroup for stateful tests.

failure-mode.description: Hardcoding auth tokens or credentials in test fixtures and committing to git.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Leaked tokens are a security incident. Run a scrubber pass before every commit.

failure-mode.description: Calling the live external service (Stripe, SendGrid) in tests because it works locally.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CI environments lack external credentials; tests become flaky. Mock at the HTTP boundary with respx/WireMock.

failure-mode.description: Importing pactman on the provider side.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: pactman is unmaintained. Use pact-python>=2 for all provider verification.
